title: Xenial Velvety Dutch Dumpling Skewers
description: Charred, velvety skewered dumplings glazed with oyster sauce, perfect for a low‑calorie sick‑day Dutch lunch.
instructions: Preheat a nonstick skillet over medium-high heat.
In a bowl, combine 1 cup whole wheat flour, 1 tsp salt, and 2 tbsp water to make a pliable dough; knead until smooth.
Divide dough into 12 equal portions; roll each into a 4cm circle.
Place 1 tablespoon diced silken tofu, 1/2 tsp minced garlic, 1/4 tsp grated ginger, and 1 tsp sliced scallion onto the center of each circle; fold dough over to form a dumpling and pinch to seal.
Thread each dumpling onto a pre-soaked bamboo skewer, leaving a small gap between them.
Add 1 tsp sesame oil to the skillet and lay the skewered dumplings skin side down; sear for 2 minutes until charred edges appear.
Flip the skewers and sear the other side for 1 minute.
Whisk together 2 tbsp oyster sauce, 1 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tsp honey, and 1 tsp cornstarch dissolved in 1 tbsp water to create a velvety glaze.
Reduce heat to low, pour glaze over the skewered dumplings, and simmer for 2 minutes until the sauce thickens and coats the dumplings.
Garnish with extra sliced scallion and serve immediately.
